page: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel x86_64 processor. It features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 256 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including Intel Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated vector and matrix operations. This instance does not include local storage or GPUs.

Benchmark data shows strong performance in memory-intensive operations, with uncached read memory bandwidth reaching 208.51 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 99.59 GB/sec, both placing in the top 25% tier. Static web serving throughput is also strong at 44.59 GB/sec. In computational tasks, the instance delivers average performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1880.0 and a multi-core score of 15821.0. Image and text processing benchmarks show strong results, while cryptographic and database operations perform in the average range, with Redis SET operations executing at 36.28 million operations per second.

Given its high memory density and strong memory bandwidth, the n4-highmem-32 is highly cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require local SSD storage or GPU acceleration. It is well-suited for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, enterprise web serving, and text or image processing pipelines. The inclusion of AMX and AVX-512 instructions also makes it capable of handling CPU-based machine learning inference, as demonstrated by its strong text generation speeds on smaller language models.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 is a memory-optimized server configured with 32 dedicated Intel x86_64 vCPUs and 256 GB of RAM, yielding 8 GB of memory per core. It features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance does not include local storage or GPU resources. In benchmark testing, it demonstrates strong memory performance, with uncached read bandwidth of 208.51 GB/sec and write bandwidth of 99.59 GB/sec, alongside strong static web serving throughput of 44.59 GB/sec. Computational and database benchmarks, including Redis SET operations at 36.28 million operations per second, fall within the average tier. This configuration is highly efficient for memory-intensive workloads, including in-memory databases, caching layers, enterprise web serving, and text or image processing pipelines.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 is an x86_64 server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and Intel AMX support. It delivers strong memory bandwidth and is optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-highmem-32 features 32 vCPUs and 256 GB RAM, offering strong memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high memory bandwidth and advanced matrix extensions for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated Intel vCPUs and 256 GB RAM providing 8 GB per core
Strong uncached memory read bandwidth of 208.51 GB/sec
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Strong static web serving throughput of 44.59 GB/sec
